info.title: PG Atlas API
info.description:
Permissionless REST API exposing the **PG Atlas** graph dataset with top-down
coverage of [Stellar Community Fund](https://communityfund.stellar.org/) projects,
and gradual bottom-up coverage of the Stellar software ecosystem.

## What is PG Atlas?

PG Atlas maps the software supply chain of SCF-funded projects — repositories,
dependencies, contributors, and health metrics — into a queryable graph.  This
API is the primary interface for the
[PG Atlas Dashboard](https://github.com/SCF-Public-Goods-Maintenance/pg-atlas-frontend)
and the
[PG Atlas TypeScript SDK](https://github.com/SCF-Public-Goods-Maintenance/pg-atlas-ts-sdk).

## Data sources

Every endpoint is tagged with the upstream source(s) its data originates from
(`OpenGrants`, `deps.dev`, `GitHub`, or `PG Atlas` for computed metrics).

## Contributing data

You can add your own project's data to the Atlas by adopting the
[SBOM Action](https://github.com/SCF-Public-Goods-Maintenance/pg-atlas-sbom-action).
This is how we increase the visibility of the Stellar ecosystem, particularly when
it comes to proprietary (private) codebases and non-SCF funded projects.

If your project is already visible in the graph, its quality is still limited by
what we can publicly crawl. By adopting the SBOM Action in all your git repositories
that build on Stellar, you'll contribute to improving the completeness and the
quality of the data that PG Atlas exposes.

## Authentication

Read endpoints are **public** — no authentication required.  Write endpoints
under `/ingest` require a GitHub Actions OIDC token.

## Versioning

This API is currently **unversioned** (development phase).  See
[api-versioning.md](https://github.com/SCF-Public-Goods-Maintenance/pg-atlas-backend/blob/main/pg_atlas/routers/api-versioning.md)
for the future versioning strategy.

get.description:
Return the current health status and application version.

This endpoint is intentionally dependency-free (no DB call) so that it
remains responsive even when the database is unreachable. A richer check
with named component statuses (db, graph) will be added in A2.

post.description: Accepts an SPDX 2.3 JSON SBOM document from the pg-atlas-sbom-action. Requires a valid GitHub OIDC Bearer token with `aud` set to the PG Atlas API URL. The submitting repository is identified from the token's `repository` claim — no additional configuration is required in the calling repo beyond `permissions: id-token: write`.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of SBOM submission records.  Supports optional filtering by ``repository`` claim and pagination via ``limit`` / ``offset`` query parameters.  Does not require authentication.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of items to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return

get.description: Returns a single SBOM submission record along with the raw artifact content read from the backing store.  If the artifact file is missing, the ``raw_artifact`` field is ``null``.  Does not require authentication.

Return aggregate counts across all graph entities.

Counts are computed on-the-fly via simple ``COUNT(*)`` queries.  No
caching is applied in this dev version — queries hit the DB directly.

Paginated list of SCF-funded projects with optional filters.

- **project_type**: filter by `public-good` or `scf-project`.
- **activity_status**: filter by lifecycle status (`live`, `in-dev`, etc.).
- **search**: case-insensitive substring match on `display_name`.
- Results are ordered by `canonical_id` for deterministic pagination.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of items to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return

get.description:
Full detail for a single project, including validated metadata.

The ``metadata`` field is the normalised form of the raw JSONB column —
unknown keys from the crawler are passed through via ``extra="allow"``.

Paginated list of repos that belong to the given project.

Returns 404 if the project does not exist.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of items to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return

get.description: Paginated contributors aggregated across all repos belonging to the project.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of items to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return

get.description:
Collapsed project-level dependencies.

Aggregates repo-level ``depends_on`` edges: for each distinct target project,
returns the target project summary and the number of repo-level edges between
the two projects.  Self-references and edges to external repos (which have no
project) are excluded.

get.summary: Projects that depend on this project
get.description:
Collapsed project-level reverse dependencies.

Same aggregation as ``depends-on`` but in the reverse direction: which
other projects have repos that depend on repos of *this* project.

Paginated list of in-ecosystem repos with optional filters.

- **project_id**: filter to repos belonging to a specific project.
- **search**: case-insensitive substring match on `display_name`.
- Results are ordered by `canonical_id` for deterministic pagination.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of items to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return

get.description:
List of direct dependencies (outgoing edges) for a given repo.

Each entry includes the target vertex's canonical ID, display name,
type (``repo`` or ``external-repo``), version range, and confidence level.

get.summary: Repos that depend on this repo
get.description:
List of direct dependents (incoming edges) for a given repo.

Each entry includes the source vertex's canonical ID, display name,
type, version range, and confidence level.
Paginated contributors for one repo with commit-count and commit-date spans.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of items to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return

get.description:
Full detail for a single repo including parent project, contributors,
releases, and dependency counts.
Paginated contributor list with optional case-insensitive name filtering.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of items to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return

get.description:
Full detail for a single contributor with aggregated statistics and
per-repo commit activity.

The ``total_commits`` field sums commits across all repos.
``first_contribution`` / ``last_contribution`` are the earliest and latest
commit dates across all repos.
Paginated list of gitlog processing attempts with optional repo filter.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of items to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return

get.description: Return one gitlog attempt record with its raw artifact content when available.

Visibility.title: Visibility
Visibility.description: Whether a Repo is publicly accessible (affects data collection scope).

SubmissionStatus.title: SubmissionStatus
SubmissionStatus.description: Processing state of an artifact-linked audit record.

ScfSubmission.title: ScfSubmission
ScfSubmission.description: A single SCF funding round submission.

SbomSubmissionResponse.title: SbomSubmissionResponse
SbomSubmissionResponse.description:
Full SBOM submission record.

Serialised from the ``SbomSubmission`` ORM model.  The ``sbom_content_hash``
field is the SHA-256 hex digest of the raw submitted payload.

SbomSubmissionListResponse.title: SbomSubmissionListResponse
SbomSubmissionListResponse.description:
Paginated list of SBOM submission records.

Returned by the list endpoint with ``total`` reflecting the count after
any ``repository`` filter has been applied.

SbomSubmissionDetailResponse.title: SbomSubmissionDetailResponse
SbomSubmissionDetailResponse.description:
Extended SBOM submission record with the raw artifact content.

Returned by the detail endpoint.  ``raw_artifact`` contains the full JSON
text of the stored SBOM, or ``None`` if the artifact file is missing from
the store.

SbomAcceptedResponse.title: SbomAcceptedResponse
SbomAcceptedResponse.description: Response body returned on successful SBOM submission (202 Accepted).

RepoVertexType.title: RepoVertexType
RepoVertexType.description: Discriminator values for the RepoVertex joined-table-inheritance hierarchy.

RepoSummary.title: RepoSummary
RepoSummary.description:
Compact repo representation used in list endpoints and as an embedded
reference in project detail responses.

RepoDetailResponse.title: RepoDetailResponse
RepoDetailResponse.description:
Full repo detail with parent project, contributors, releases, dependency counts,
and active contributor stats.

DepCounts.title: DepCounts
DepCounts.description:
Counts of dependency edges grouped by target vertex type.

Provided as part of the repo detail response so the frontend can show
"depends on N repos + M external" without fetching the full edge list.

ContributorSummary.title: ContributorSummary
ContributorSummary.description: Compact contributor reference embedded in repo detail responses.

ActivityStatus.title: ActivityStatus

ProjectType.title: ProjectType
ProjectType.description: Classification of a Project within the PG Atlas universe.

ProjectSummary.title: ProjectSummary
ProjectSummary.description:
Compact project representation used in list endpoints and as an embedded
reference in repo detail responses.

RepoDependency.title: RepoDependency
RepoDependency.description:
A single dependency or reverse-dependency edge from a repo.

``vertex_type`` tells the frontend whether the target is an in-ecosystem
``Repo`` or an ``ExternalRepo``.

EdgeConfidence.title: EdgeConfidence
EdgeConfidence.description: How firmly an edge was established.

RepoContributorSummary.title: RepoContributorSummary
RepoContributorSummary.description: Contributor summary with commit counts for one repo.

ProjectMetadata.title: ProjectMetadata
ProjectMetadata.description:
Validates and normalises the ``project_metadata`` JSONB column on ``Project``.

The single write path (``_build_project_metadata`` in the OpenGrants crawler)
guarantees consistent key names and types.  ``extra = "allow"`` ensures that
any future keys added by the crawler pass through without breaking the API.
Future keys must still be added in this model for clarity.

ProjectDetailResponse.title: ProjectDetailResponse
ProjectDetailResponse.description:
Full project detail including contributor stats and metadata.

``metadata`` is the normalised form of the ``project_metadata`` JSONB column.

ProjectDependency.title: ProjectDependency
ProjectDependency.description:
A collapsed project-level dependency: aggregates repo-level edges
between two projects into a single summary.

ProjectContributorSummary.title: ProjectContributorSummary
ProjectContributorSummary.description: Contributor summary aggregated across all repos in a project.

GitLogArtifactSummary.title: GitLogArtifactSummary
GitLogArtifactSummary.description: Compact gitlog artifact audit record for list endpoints.

GitLogArtifactDetailResponse.title: GitLogArtifactDetailResponse
GitLogArtifactDetailResponse.description: Full gitlog artifact audit record including raw artifact content.

ContributorDetailResponse.title: ContributorDetailResponse
ContributorDetailResponse.description: Full contributor detail with aggregated statistics and per-repo activity.

ContributionEntry.title: ContributionEntry
ContributionEntry.description: A single repo that a contributor has committed to.
